learnings: min-mem alone achieves 19.3% char reduction with 96.1% noun retention at **zero inference cost**.
llmlingua-2@0.5 reaches 42.3% char savings but noun retention drops to 82.0% (jaccard 0.68).
gpt2-selective@0.5 reaches 33.2% char savings but noun retention drops to 66.3% (jaccard 0.66).
min-mem → GPT-2 selective stacking yields higher total compression than either stage alone at the same keep ratio.
**Contribution framing:** min-mem is the auditable, zero-cost normalization tier; LM deletion methods are optional second stages for byte-starved deployments.
Retrieval QA: `identity` scored 100% (+0 pts vs verbose) with 0% memory reduction.
